data.title: Meta to cease political ads in the EU due to incoming TTPA regulation
data.description:
Meta (NASDAQ:META) said Friday it will stop showing political, electoral, and social issue advertisements across its platforms in the European Union starting in October this year.

The move by the social media and tech giant comes ahead of the bloc's "Transparency and Targeting of Political Advertising" (TTPA) regulation, which introduces "unworkable requirements and legal uncertainties," according to the company.

Meta believes TTPA will restrict how advertisers reach their audiences, and people will also see less relevant content.

"Unfortunately, the TTPA introduces significant, additional obligations to our processes and systems that create an untenable level of complexity and legal uncertainty for advertisers and platforms operating in the EU," Meta said in its blog post.

"For example, the TTPA places extensive restrictions on ad targeting and delivery, which would restrict how political and social issue advertisers can reach their audiences and lead to people seeing less relevant ads on our platforms. It is yet another threat to the principles of personalized advertising, ignoring the benefits to advertisers and the people they want to reach," they added.

The introduction of TTPA won’t prevent people or candidates in the EU from posting about and debating politics on the company's platforms, Meta clarified.

data.title: 5 stocks to watch on Friday: PSX, EW, NEM, GILD, CNC
data.description:
Stock index futures ticked higher on Friday, a day after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite hit fresh record high closes. Here are five stocks to watch out for:

Phillips 66 (PSX) shares were up 2.2% in premarket trade after the company beat both its top and bottom line estimates in the second quarter. The firm benefited from reduced turnaround expenses and higher refining margins. 

Edwards Lifesciences (EW) stock was up 8.7% before the opening bell as it upped its full-year outlook after its Q2 results exceeded expectations for both profit and revenue. The company has updated its sales growth guidance for 2025, now projecting an increase of 9-10%, up from the previous estimate of 8-10%.

Newmont (NEM) shares were up 2.6% in premarket trade after it reported higher Q2 profit than a year ago that also easily beat analyst expectations, as the world's top publicly traded gold miner benefited from the rally in bullion prices in recent months.

Gilead (GILD) stock rose 0.8% after an expert panel of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency as part of an accelerated review, endorsed its antiviral therapy lenacapavir as a twice-yearly treatment for prevention of HIV in pre-exposure prophylaxis.

Centene (CNC) shares will be in focus after the Medicaid-driven managed care firm fell short of earnings expectations with its Q2 2025 results, recording its first quarterly earnings miss in four years.

data.title: Neinor Homes posts H1 revenue of €148mn, reaffirms FY25 targets
data.description:
• Neinor Homes (OTCPK:NNRHF) posted its results for the first half of 2025 on Friday.
  • Total revenues amounted to €148mn, of which the core Build-to-Sell (BTS) business contributed €112mn through the delivery of 323 units at an average selling price (ASP) of €348,000.
  • The asset management platform contributed €9mn, while ancillary activities including land sales, rental income, and construction services generated an additional €27mn.
  • The company achieved a 30.6% gross margin, supported by a favourable product mix, disciplined pricing strategy, and tight control over construction costs.
  • This translated into a gross profit of €45mn and an EBITDA of €18mn. At the bottom line, adjusted net income stood at €6mn, reflecting the expected concentration of deliveries in the second half of the year.
  • Neinor reiterated its FY25 targets with bulk of deliveries scheduled to take place during 2H25 as follows:
    • Deliveries: c.2,000
    • Total Revenues: €600–700mn (ASP: €375,000–400,000 per unit)
    • EBITDA: €100–110mn (c.28% gross margin)
  • During the first half, the company pre-sold 1,701 housing units, representing a 45% year-over-year increase. These transactions generated a total economic value of €579mn, with an ASP of €340,000 per unit.
  • Within Neinor’s fully owned BTS portfolio, pre-sales reached 957 units worth €348mn (ASP: €364,000 per unit), marking a +20% increase over the same period last year. In addition, Neinor sold 251 units from its Build-to-Rent (BTR) portfolio, including the Delta and Seville developments to Round Hill Capital, as well as part of the Sardes portfolio to 1810 Capital.
  • As of June 30, Neinor’s total managed orderbook stood at a record 4,520 housing units, representing €1.63bn in future revenues. Of these, 2,274 units belong to Neinor’s fully owned portfolio and had an economic value of €824mn (ASP: €363,000 per unit).
  • Neinor closed the semester managing an active portfolio of 10,762 units, up 57% year-over-year.
  • As of June 30, Neinor’s total managed land bank reached 22,400 units.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Union Pacific upped to buy at Jefferies on Norfolk Southern deal talks
data.description:
Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) was raised to buy from hold at Jefferies after the railroad operator confirmed it was in talks with Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) about a business combination.

Investors should start building a position in Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) to "get in on the ground level" on what is likely a "transformational" combination, Jefferies analyst Stephanie Moore wrote in a note on Friday. Moore raised her UNP price target to $285 from $250. 

"We also acknowledge that a proposed deal would face significant risks—regulatory approval from STB as well as execution risk related to integrating two large rail operations," Moore wrote. "Ultimately, however, we think major stakeholders are aligned towards the benefits of a transcontinental deal and see the current administration as being supportive, given the potential boost to US infrastructure."

Moore also cut her rating on Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) to hold from buy, citing the recent rally in NSC shares amid reports about a possible combination. 

"We think shares have largely already priced in a potential acquisition premium and think upside from here looks limited," Moore, who has a $300 price target on NSC, wrote in the note. "Given regulatory uncertainty and the risk of a failed bid, we recommend holding NSC rather than initiating new positions at current levels." 

Jefferies Moore also recommended CSX Corp. (CSX) shares given an increased likelihood of a BNSF-CSX combination.

A "BNSF-CSX combination would create a rival transcontinental network, effectively reshaping the U.S. rail industry into a duopoly," Moore, who has a $40 price target on CSX, added.

CSX CEO Joe Hinrichs said this week that he's  open to merger talks with other railroad operators after recent reports of a potential Norfolk Southern/Union Pacific deal.

data.title: Boston Beer gains after the company shows it can mitigate some tariff pain through productivity initiatives
data.description:
Boston Beer (NYSE:SAM) rallied in premarket trading on Friday after the beer company issued better-than-feared guidance alongside its Q2 earnings report.

Boston Beer sees EPS of $8.00 to $10.50 (midpoint $9.25) vs. $8.96 consensus. The updates to the company's full-year 2025 financial guidance reflect lower volume expectations driven by macroeconomic factors impacting industry demand and higher gross margin expectations driven by better-than-expected brewery efficiencies in the first half of the year. The EPS guidance was unchanged from an operating performance perspective and includes a revised estimated negative impact from tariffs. "Significant progress on our ongoing productivity initiatives has been key to raising our gross margin guidance even as we absorb the impact of tariffs," updated CFO Diego Reynoso.

The negative impact of tariffs for Boston Beer (NYSE:SAM) is expected to be more significant in the second half of the year.

Shares of Boston Beer (SAM) were up 9.5% in premarket trading to $221.01 to swap hands at their highest level since the first week of June.

data.title: Centene in selloff after rare earnings miss; sets outlook below consensus
data.description:
Shares of Centene (NYSE:CNC) lost ~10% in the premarket on Friday after the Medicaid-driven managed care firm fell short of earnings expectations with its Q2 2025 results, recording its first quarterly earnings miss in four years.

Having pulled its full-year outlook early this month, CNC set its 2025 earnings per share guidance at $1.75 during the earnings call, compared to $2.94 in the consensus, assuming a $2.4B hit from worse-than-expected cost trends in its Affordable Care Act (ACA) Marketplace business.

Centene’s (NYSE:CNC) peers, Molina Healthcare (MOH) and Elevance Health (ELV), which had reported their Q2 results over the past few days, also slid in reaction alongside Oscar Health (OSCR), another player in the Medicaid space.

While Saint Louis, Missouri-based Centene (NYSE:CNC) reported $48.7B in revenue for the quarter, exceeding the consensus by as much as $4.5B, the company swung to a loss per share of $0.16 in Q2 compared to $2.42 of earnings per share in the prior year quarter on an adjusted basis.

"We are disappointed by our second quarter results, but we have a clear understanding of the trends that have impacted our performance and are working with urgency and focus to restore our earnings trajectory,” CEO Sarah London said.

Rising medical costs appear to have driven the quarterly loss, as the company’s health benefits ratio reached 93.0% in Q2, up from 87.6% in the year-ago period and compared to 91.6% in the consensus, according to Bloomberg data.

Centene (NYSE:CNC) attributed the MBR spike mainly to adverse cost trends in its ACA Marketplace and Medicaid businesses.

"Despite the shifting landscape, we believe that the staying power of Medicaid, Medicare, and the Individual Marketplace is as strong as it has ever been,” CEO London added.

Update 8.45 AM ET: Adds comments on the guidance
